meaning: n.(尤指道德或精神方面的)教诲,启迪,熏陶
examples: The books were intended for the edification of the masses.
Demonstrations, films, and videotapes are shown for your edification.
Thanks to the support of Love, Respect, Accompanier and Edification, the children will have brilliant future.
Does it stack up as the ultimate lowbrow carny-sideshow of a cultural exposition or is it perhaps a form of stealth edification and ennoblement?
The edification conveyed from old name to new: His older brother, John, standing on a chair, holding aloft a dented tin cup brimful with water.
